textPlain: Removing choice is fine, but people will want to know what number they'll get if they order right now. Even if you can't or won't show the next rock, show the next number.
$50 for a rock is outrageous. I would hope that it at least comes with a cool sticker, then I would be willing to accept it.
It is great they are documenting each rock. I would hate to get a forgery.
Are those pet rocks or wild rocks? Are they homebroken? Can they be a home rock as I won't be able to take them for walks daily.
What kind of scam is this?
This kind of objectification is stone cold.
Like physical EtherRocks[0]. Do will also come with a digital certificate that can be transfered online?[0] https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/EtherRock
This will pair very well with my Anthropologie rock.

That's actually kinda hard, no?Say the server has a counter. When you load the page, it's at 57, so it displays that you would be ordering #57. While you're looking at this, someone else loads the page - what number do you show them? If you show 57, then whoever orders first gets it and the other person gets a message "Sorry, not available. Want 58 instead?" but the same thing could then happen to them with #58, too – "Sorry, not available. Want 59 instead?"So maybe instead you show the 2nd person counter+1, i.e. 58. And you show the 3rd person counter+2, i.e. 59. But what if #59 purchases but 57 and 58 don't? What do you show the NEXT person, 57 or 60?I'm not saying it's intractable but it merits thought.
I agree - even adjusting for inflation the original Pet Rock [1] wouldn't have cost more than about $25. /s[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pet_Rock
